# ADR-004: Whiteboarding / Draw Mode (Excalidraw → custom React Flow tools)

- **Status:** Superseded in practice (see "Current State")
- **Date:** 2025
- **Deciders:** Core team

## Context

The canvas has two environments (see [ADR-007](./ADR-007:%20Adding%20XState.md)): a **practical** environment for structured metric-tree editing (React Flow nodes/edges) and a **design** environment for free-form whiteboarding (sketching, shapes, annotations). The original intent was to adopt **Excalidraw** to power the design environment.

## Decision (original)

Adopt Excalidraw for the whiteboard/"design" mode, giving users hand-drawn-style sketching and shapes alongside the structured canvas.

## Current State (what was actually built)

The implementation diverged from the original decision:

- **Excalidraw is not an active component.** Only its CSS is imported (`src/main.tsx`); no Excalidraw React component is mounted.
- Whiteboarding is instead a **custom drawing system layered over React Flow**, in `src/features/canvas/components/whiteboard/`:
  - `WhiteboardContainer.tsx` manages tool state (`select`, `freehand`, `rectangle`, `lasso`, `erase`), brush size, and color.
  - `FreehandDrawComponent`, `RectangleToolComponent`, `LassoSelectionComponent`, `EraseToolComponent` implement the tools via pointer events.
- The active environment (`practical` vs `design`) is driven by the XState canvas machine; design tools render when `currentEnvironment === 'design'`.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Excalidraw (as integrated component)** — Original choice; in practice it was heavy to co-locate with React Flow's coordinate/viewport system and was reduced to a styling dependency.
- **tldraw** — Another full whiteboard engine; same coexistence concerns with React Flow.
- **Custom React Flow drawing layer** — Chosen in practice: keeps a single canvas/viewport model and full control over interaction.

## Consequences

- One canvas/viewport system to reason about (React Flow), at the cost of building/maintaining drawing tools ourselves.
- The Excalidraw CSS import is now effectively vestigial and is a candidate for cleanup.
- This ADR is retained (rather than deleted) to record *why* the title says "Excalidraw" but the code does not use it.

## References

- `src/main.tsx` (Excalidraw CSS import only)
- `src/features/canvas/components/whiteboard/`
- `src/lib/machines/canvasMachine.ts`, `docs/editor/DRAW_MODE_ARCHITECTURE.md`

content.text:
Both fixes are done and verified.

## Fixed

**1. Removed the broken `TypedProjectForm.tsx`**
- It imported `useTypedValidation` (`useProjectForm`) — a hook that was deleted in the feature-structure refactor — and was referenced nowhere. Deleted it.
- While confirming, I found *why* it never surfaced as an error: `npm run type-check` runs `tsc --noEmit` against the root `tsconfig.json`, which has `"files": []` + `references`. Plain `tsc` (not `tsc -b`) on that config compiles **nothing** — so your type-check is effectively a no-op and wouldn't catch a broken import anywhere in `src`. Flagging it; the fix would be `tsc -b --noEmit`, but that's a separate decision (it'll likely surface other latent errors).

**2. Removed the vestigial Excalidraw CSS**
- Deleted the `@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css` import from `src/main.tsx`.
- Also removed a dead companion CSS rule in `src/styles/index.css` (`.excalidraw-container-active .excalidraw`) — its selector is applied to no element.
- Verified there's **no** JS/TS import of `@excalidraw` anywhere; the "Excalidraw setTool" calls in `CanvasPage.tsx` are on your custom `whiteboardRef` (`WhiteboardContainer`), just mislabeled in log strings.

**3. Updated docs to match** — the Prisma guide/quick-ref and ADR-004/ADR-006 now describe these as removed (past tense) rather than "broken/cleanup candidate."

**Verified:** no source references to the deleted items remain, and the docs site still builds clean (`build complete in 6.75s`, zero dead links).

Two small leftovers I did **not** touch (your call):
- `@excalidraw/excalidraw` is now an **unused dependency** in `package.json` — safe to `npm uninstall`, but it churns `package-lock.json` so I left it (noted in ADR-004).
- Stale "Excalidraw" wording in canvas comments/log strings (`CanvasPage.tsx`, `canvasMachine.ts`, `useCanvasViewportSync.ts`) — cosmetic only.

Want me to drop the unused dependency and/or commit everything (docs + these source fixes)?

content.text:
App builds clean (`✓ built in 12.49s` — the chunk-size warning is pre-existing and unrelated). All cleanups verified.

## This round — code cleanup, done & verified
- **Removed `@excalidraw/excalidraw`** dependency (fully unused).
- **Deleted dead viewport-sync logic** — the `source === 'excalidraw'` guard in `useCanvasViewportSync.ts` could never be true (type is `'reactflow' | null`), so it was dead code; removing it preserves behavior exactly.
- **Renamed stale "Excalidraw" wording** in `CanvasPage.tsx` (comments + log strings) and `canvasMachine.ts` to "whiteboard", so the code no longer implies a package it doesn't use.
- (From before: deleted broken `TypedProjectForm.tsx`, removed the CSS import + dead `.excalidraw-container-active` rule.)

Confirmed: zero `excalidraw` references remain in `src`, app build passes, docs build passes.

## Where things stand
All the work you asked for is complete:
- ✅ 8 ADRs filled (+ ADR index)
- ✅ Prisma/Zod consolidated (4→2, real API/paths)
- ✅ EditorJS docs merged (2→1)
- ✅ Auth external copies → custom project guide
- ✅ Code cleanup (Excalidraw + dead TypedProjectForm)

**I think we're done** — unless you want to tackle the one latent issue I found along the way: `npm run type-check` (`tsc --noEmit` on a `"files": []` root config) currently checks **nothing**, so broken imports anywhere in `src` go unnoticed. Fixing it to `tsc -b --noEmit` would restore real type-checking but will likely surface pre-existing errors to work through.

There's now a sizable uncommitted changeset (docs + source). Want me to:

content.text: The 819 generated errors are systematic: a **stale/partial Prisma generation** — missing `./enums/` modules (TS2307) and references to `Prisma.usersSelect`/`usersFindFirstArgs` that the current client doesn't expose (TS2694). The actually-imported `objects/*` schemas are valid Zod at runtime, but the generated *type* layer is out of date. The clean fix is to **regenerate** — and `prisma generate` reads `schema.prisma` only (no live DB needed). Let me try it:

content.text:
Generated dir fully restored to committed state. I have the complete picture now, and it's materially bigger than "a few pre-existing errors" — I need to surface this before grinding for hours.

## What turning on real type-checking reveals

`tsc -b` (real project-references check) surfaces **~1,750 errors**, in two very different buckets:

**Bucket A — ~1,500 errors in auto-generated Prisma/Zod code.** This is a `prisma-zod-generator@1.4.1` ↔ strict-TS / `@prisma/client@6.13.0` incompatibility — the generator emits runtime-valid Zod that doesn't pass strict `tsc` (references `Prisma.usersSelect`-style types the client doesn't expose, missing `enums/` modules). I tested regenerating: it made it **worse** (1,500 → ~1,500+, and churned 175 files), so this is structural, not stale output. **These can't be hand-fixed sanely** — they have to be *excluded* from type-checking (cleanest: a post-generate step stamping `// @ts-nocheck`, so it survives regen). I've reverted my regen experiment; generated dir is back to committed state.

**Bucket B — ~250 real errors in hand-written app code** (canvas, services, nodes). Accumulated type debt that's been invisible because type-check was a no-op. Roughly:
- **~80 mechanical & safe:** 56 unused vars (TS6133), 24 implicit-any params (TS7006).
- **~170 real type mismatches:** property-doesn't-exist (47), no-overload-matches (27), arg-not-assignable (26), not-assignable (16), etc. These need per-site judgment and **carry real regression risk** — fixing a type wrong can change behavior, and I can't fully runtime-verify this app.

The honest read: this is a **multi-hundred-error remediation plus a generator-tooling fix**, not a quick toggle. And flipping the `type-check` script now turns `npm run build` **red** until every one is resolved.

Given that — how far do you want to take this? (This is a real scope/risk call, which is why I'm checking rather than just grinding.)

input.command:

> /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/fix_files.txt
> /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/quarantine_files.txt
while read -r f; do
  total=$(grep -F "$f(" "$r" | wc -l)
  risky=$(grep -F "$f(" "$r" | grep -vcE "TS6133|TS7006")
  if [ "$risky" -eq 0 ]; then echo "$f ($total safe)" >> /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/fix_files.txt
  else echo "$f ($total total, $risky risky)" >> /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/quarantine_files.txt; fi
done < /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/all_files.txt
echo "=== FIX IN PLACE (only-safe errors): $(wc -l < /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/fix_files.txt) files ==="
cat /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/fix_files.txt
echo ""
echo "=== QUARANTINE (@ts-nocheck): $(wc -l < /home/nadeemramli/.claude/jobs/54883d88/tmp/quarantine_files.txt) files ==="

## Summary

I fixed all 33 listed `TS6133` errors with minimal changes. Files changed (19 total):

**Unused imports removed:**
- `src/features/canvas/components/edges/shared/EnhancedEdgeButton.tsx` — removed `Separator` import (also dropped unused destructured props `edgeId`, `confidence`)
- `src/features/canvas/components/panels/node-settings/MetricNodeSettings.tsx` — removed `Trash2`
- `src/features/canvas/stores/useCanvasNodesStore.ts` — removed `useAppStore`
- `src/stories/Button.tsx`, `src/stories/Header.tsx` — removed `import React from 'react'`

**Unused destructured props / hook returns dropped:**
- `AssetsPage.tsx` (`setProject`), `CanvasStateMachineProvider.tsx` (`canvasId`), `CanvasModals.tsx` (param → `_props`), `QuickSearchCommand.tsx` (`onResultSelect`)
- `EnhancedCanvasPage.tsx` — `canvasId`, `isWhiteboardActive`, `designElements`, `designAppState`, `viewport`

**Unused locals removed:**
- `CommentSummaryDashboard.tsx` (`totalComments`), `CanvasSettingsPage.tsx` (`getTimeAgo`), `edgeConnectionRules.ts` (`edgesWithProposed`)
- `TopCanvasToolbar.tsx` — removed `keepToolActive`/`onToggleKeepToolActive`/`drawActiveTool`/`onSetDrawTool` from destructuring and the unused `drawTools` array

**Unused params prefixed with `_`:**
- `DataFlowEdge.tsx`/`ReferenceEdge.tsx` (`id` removed — first positional, no later positional deps), `chart-node.tsx` (`_entry`), `data-transformation-node.tsx` (`_config`), `ChangelogTab.tsx` (`_action`)
- `data-transformation-node.tsx` — `basicTransformations`/`sourceData` state: dropped the unused getter, kept the setter (`const [, setX] = useState(...)`)

**Cascade fixes** (to avoid introducing new TS6133 errors from the above removals, none changing runtime behavior):
- `TopCanvasToolbar.tsx` — removed 6 lucide icons (`ArrowRight, Circle, Diamond, Image, Minus, Type`) that only the deleted `drawTools` array used
- `EnhancedCanvasPage.tsx` — removed the now-orphaned `handleWhiteboardChange` callback, its sole dependency `updateDesignData` from destructuring, and the now-unused `useCallback` import
- `edgeConnectionRules.ts` — `targetId` → `_targetId` (it was only referenced by the dead `edgesWithProposed` block)

**Final `tsc -b` (filtered, excluding prisma/generated): 4 errors remaining, all in `src/tests/xstate-react-integration.test.tsx`** (explicitly out of scope / handled separately):

input.content:
# Type-Check Debt

This page tracks the files that were **quarantined** when strict type-checking was turned on, so the debt is explicit and dischargeable rather than silently ignored.

## Background

`npm run type-check` used to run `tsc --noEmit` against the root `tsconfig.json`, which has `"files": []` + project `references`. Plain `tsc` (not `tsc -b`) on that config compiles **nothing**, so type errors anywhere in `src` went unnoticed.

It now runs **`tsc -b --noEmit`**, which actually checks the app. Turning that on surfaced two kinds of pre-existing errors:

1. **Auto-generated Prisma/Zod code (~1,500 errors).** `prisma-zod-generator` emits runtime-valid Zod whose *types* don't pass strict `tsc` against the installed `@prisma/client`. These files are stamped `// @ts-nocheck` automatically by `scripts/stamp-generated-nocheck.mjs`, which runs as part of `npm run prisma:generate` — so the stamp survives every regeneration. **Do not hand-edit generated files.**

2. **Hand-written app code (~250 errors).** The mechanically-safe ones (unused symbols, implicit-any params) were **fixed**. The remaining ones — **219 errors across 37 files** — are real type mismatches that need per-site judgment and carry regression risk, so each such file was stamped `// @ts-nocheck` with a `TODO(type-debt)` pointer to this page.

> ⚠️ A quarantined (`@ts-nocheck`) file is **entirely unchecked**, including any *new* code added to it. Treat the list below as "fix and un-quarantine", and avoid piling new logic into these files.

## How to discharge debt for a file

1. Remove the `// @ts-nocheck` + `TODO(type-debt)` header from the top of the file.
2. Run `npm run type-check` and fix the errors it reports for that file.
3. Verify behavior (the app/build, relevant tests) before committing — many of these are `TS2339`/`TS2769`/`TS2345` mismatches where a wrong "fix" can change runtime behavior.
4. Delete the file's row from the table below.

## Prioritize: likely-real broken imports (`TS2307`)

`TS2307: Cannot find module` usually means an actually-broken import path (not just loose typing) — these are worth fixing first:

- `lib/workers/compute.worker.ts` (2)
- `features/canvas/components/CanvasDebugPanels.tsx`, `features/canvas/components/CanvasModals.tsx`, `features/canvas/components/panels/chart-panel/chart-builder.tsx`, `shared/lib/supabase/services/relationships.ts`, `shared/lib/supabase/services/version-history.ts` (1 each)

toolUseResult.content:
# Type-Check Debt

This page tracks the files that were **quarantined** when strict type-checking was turned on, so the debt is explicit and dischargeable rather than silently ignored.

## Background

`npm run type-check` used to run `tsc --noEmit` against the root `tsconfig.json`, which has `"files": []` + project `references`. Plain `tsc` (not `tsc -b`) on that config compiles **nothing**, so type errors anywhere in `src` went unnoticed.

It now runs **`tsc -b --noEmit`**, which actually checks the app. Turning that on surfaced two kinds of pre-existing errors:

1. **Auto-generated Prisma/Zod code (~1,500 errors).** `prisma-zod-generator` emits runtime-valid Zod whose *types* don't pass strict `tsc` against the installed `@prisma/client`. These files are stamped `// @ts-nocheck` automatically by `scripts/stamp-generated-nocheck.mjs`, which runs as part of `npm run prisma:generate` — so the stamp survives every regeneration. **Do not hand-edit generated files.**

2. **Hand-written app code (~250 errors).** The mechanically-safe ones (unused symbols, implicit-any params) were **fixed**. The remaining ones — **219 errors across 37 files** — are real type mismatches that need per-site judgment and carry regression risk, so each such file was stamped `// @ts-nocheck` with a `TODO(type-debt)` pointer to this page.

> ⚠️ A quarantined (`@ts-nocheck`) file is **entirely unchecked**, including any *new* code added to it. Treat the list below as "fix and un-quarantine", and avoid piling new logic into these files.

## How to discharge debt for a file

1. Remove the `// @ts-nocheck` + `TODO(type-debt)` header from the top of the file.
2. Run `npm run type-check` and fix the errors it reports for that file.
3. Verify behavior (the app/build, relevant tests) before committing — many of these are `TS2339`/`TS2769`/`TS2345` mismatches where a wrong "fix" can change runtime behavior.
4. Delete the file's row from the table below.

## Prioritize: likely-real broken imports (`TS2307`)

`TS2307: Cannot find module` usually means an actually-broken import path (not just loose typing) — these are worth fixing first:

- `lib/workers/compute.worker.ts` (2)
- `features/canvas/components/CanvasDebugPanels.tsx`, `features/canvas/components/CanvasModals.tsx`, `features/canvas/components/panels/chart-panel/chart-builder.tsx`, `shared/lib/supabase/services/relationships.ts`, `shared/lib/supabase/services/version-history.ts` (1 each)

## Type-check is now real — and the build stays green

`npm run type-check` was a **no-op** (`tsc --noEmit` on a `"files": []` root config compiled nothing). It now runs **`tsc -b --noEmit`**, and `npm run build` passes end-to-end (`✓ built in 11.44s`).

### What I did (the "safe pass")
1. **Generated code (~1,500 errors) → excluded properly.** Added `scripts/stamp-generated-nocheck.mjs` that stamps `// @ts-nocheck` on the 405 generated Prisma/Zod files, and wired it into a **fixed** `prisma:generate` (it was also missing `--schema`, so regeneration was silently broken) — the stamp now survives every regen.
2. **~80 mechanical errors → actually fixed.** Unused symbols + implicit-any across **20 files** (19 via a tightly-scoped subagent, plus the test file by hand — which also had **2 genuinely broken import paths** now corrected).
3. **219 risky errors across 37 files → quarantined, not hidden.** Each got a `// @ts-nocheck` + `TODO(type-debt)` header pointing to a new tracked doc, **`docs/architecture/TYPE_CHECK_DEBT.md`** (full per-file table, error codes, discharge instructions, and a "fix these first" list of likely-real broken imports), wired into the docs sidebar.

### Verified
- `npm run build` → green (type-check + vite build)
- `npm run docs:build` → green

## Full changeset to commit
- **Docs overhaul:** 8 ADRs + ADR index, Prisma/Zod 4→2, EditorJS 2→1, auth external copies → custom guide, new type-debt doc, sidebar/config updates.
- **Code cleanup:** removed Excalidraw dep + dead CSS/code, deleted broken `TypedProjectForm.tsx`, renamed the trailing-space ADR file.
- **Type-check:** `package.json` scripts, `stamp-generated-nocheck.mjs`, 405 generated files stamped, 37 source files quarantined, 20 files fixed.

One thing to flag honestly: **405 stamped generated files** make for a large (but mechanical, one-line-each) diff. That's the cost of excluding generator output that can't be type-checked.

Want me to commit now? If so, I'd suggest **three commits** for a reviewable history: (1) docs overhaul, (2) Excalidraw/dead-code cleanup, (3) type-check enablement. Or I can do one. Your call.

docs: fill ADRs, consolidate guides, replace external auth docs

- Populate the 8 empty ADRs (001-008) + an ADR index, grounded in the actual
  codebase; record where reality diverged (Excalidraw superseded by a custom
  React Flow whiteboard; realtime/Automerge only scaffolded).
- Consolidate Prisma + Zod docs (4 files -> 2) with correct post-refactor paths
  and the real validate/service API; note the removed useTypedValidation hook.
- Merge the two EditorJS docs into one troubleshooting guide.
- Replace three verbatim external Clerk/Supabase copies with a project-specific
  integration guide.
- Add a Type-Check Debt reference page and wire it all into the VitePress nav.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "exit: $?"
git log --oneline -1
echo "=== remaining staged (should be just TypedProjectForm) ==="

Good news — my changes are all **still intact** (grep confirms: `tsc -b` script, no excalidraw, stamps present, quarantines present, `main.tsx` clean, `TypedProjectForm` deleted). The "reverted" notes were almost certainly the **husky pre-commit hook stashing my unstaged changes** to run tests, then restoring them — a transient window, not a real revert. Also, the user committed their own infra work as `c66d301` (now my commit's parent).

Let me confirm what the pre-commit hook does before committing 2 & 3 (so a stash+type-check can't fail mid-commit):

Done. All three commits landed cleanly, and I verified **none of my work is left uncommitted** (0 stray generated stamps, 0 dirty quarantine/fixed files, package/scripts clean). The 22 remaining uncommitted files are all the **user's concurrent work** (ProjectCard, WhiteboardContainer, canvasStore, AGENTS.md, vite.config.ts, BACKLOG.md, etc.) — I deliberately left those untouched.

## Committed (on `feature-organization`)

```
5bdacbb  build: enable real type-checking (tsc -b) and quarantine pre-existing debt
c9b87be  refactor: remove unused Excalidraw integration and dead code
6da4b24  docs: fill ADRs, consolidate guides, replace external auth docs
c66d301  chore: ... (your earlier commit — now the parent)
```

Each passed the husky `npm test` pre-commit hook (11 tests). I used explicit per-commit pathspecs throughout, so nothing of your in-progress work got swept in.

### Notes worth knowing
- **The tree is shared/active.** Partway through I saw `package.json`/`canvasMachine.ts`/`useCanvasViewportSync.ts` momentarily show old content — that was the husky hook stashing unstaged changes to run tests, then restoring them (not a real revert). I re-verified each file held my version before staging.
- **`tsc -b` was 0 errors at commit time** *including* your uncommitted changes — so your in-progress files currently type-check clean. Heads up though: now that `npm run build` runs `tsc -b --noEmit`, any new type error in your uncommitted work (the RLS/auth/projects files) will fail the build. The escape hatch is documented in `docs/architecture/TYPE_CHECK_DEBT.md`.
- I did **not** push — you're on `feature-organization`; push when you're ready.

That completes everything: docs overhaul, Excalidraw/dead-code cleanup, and type-check enablement — all committed, with the type debt explicitly tracked rather than hidden.
